ingredients.note: Mild glycine-based surfactant that produces soft foam at near-skin pH, common in low-irritation facial cleansers. Considered gentle and safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate
ingredients.note: Very mild anionic surfactant derived from coconut, prized for creamy lather and low irritancy in syndet bars and gentle cleansers. Considered safe and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Sodium Dehydroacetate
ingredients.note: Benign, widely used preservative authorized in the EU up to 0.6% (as acid). Low irritation and sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Sodium hyaluronate
ingredients.note: Skin-identical hydrator; safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Hyaluronate Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Cross-linked hyaluronate that forms a hydrating film at the surface; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Sodium Hydroxide
ingredients.note: Strong base used in trace amounts to neutralize acids and adjust pH. Corrosive as a raw material, but in finished products it is fully reacted and present only as a residual pH adjuster, so the product itself is safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lactate
ingredients.note: Sodium salt of lactic acid used as a buffer and humectant (NMF component). Safe and skin-conditioning.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauroyl Glutamate
ingredients.note: Glutamic-acid-derived anionic surfactant known for very mild, skin-friendly cleansing at low pH. Well tolerated with low irritation potential.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauroyl Isethionate
ingredients.note: Mild isethionate cleansing surfactant similar to SCI, common in syndet bars and facial cleansers. Low irritation and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauroyl Sarcosinate
ingredients.note: Gentle amino-acid-derived surfactant used as a mild foaming agent and in toothpaste. Low irritation; not a meaningful nitrosamine concern in modern grades.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauryl Sulfate
ingredients.note: Effective foaming cleanser and a well-known skin/eye irritant that can be drying, especially at high concentration or with prolonged contact. It is an irritant, not a toxin or endocrine disruptor, and is not absorbed to cause systemic harm.

ingredients.name: Sodium Methyl Cocoyl Taurate
ingredients.note: Mild taurine-based anionic surfactant producing dense foam, common in gentle facial cleansers. Low irritation and considered safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Methyl Oleoyl Taurate
ingredients.note: Taurine-derived mild surfactant used as a co-surfactant for creamy lather in cleansers. Gentle with low irritancy.

ingredients.name: Sodium PCA
ingredients.note: Natural moisturizing factor component and strong humectant; well tolerated and considered safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Phytate
ingredients.note: Plant-derived (rice/grain) chelator used as a natural EDTA alternative. Considered safe and biodegradable.

ingredients.name: Sodium Polyacrylate
ingredients.note: High-molecular-weight anionic polymer used as a thickener and water absorber. Safe as used; residual acrylic acid monomer is limited in cosmetic grades.

ingredients.name: Sodium Polyglutamate
ingredients.note: Salt form of polyglutamic acid used as a water-binding humectant; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Sorbic Acid
ingredients.note: Mild food-grade preservative permitted in the EU up to 0.6%. Can cause minor transient irritation but is rarely a true allergen.

ingredients.name: Sorbitan Oleate
ingredients.note: Sorbitan ester of oleic acid used as a water-in-oil co-emulsifier. Non-ethoxylated and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Sorbitan Olivate
ingredients.note: Olive-derived sorbitan ester co-emulsifier paired with cetearyl olivate. Mild and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Sorbitan Stearate
ingredients.note: Non-ethoxylated sorbitol/stearic acid ester used as a low-HLB co-emulsifier. Mild and broadly considered safe.

ingredients.name: Sorbitol
ingredients.note: Sugar-alcohol humectant similar to glycerin; benign and widely used.

ingredients.name: Squalane
ingredients.note: Lightweight, stable emollient; safe (note: 'squalane' the hydrogenated form, not unstable 'squalene').

ingredients.name: Stearic Acid
ingredients.note: Common fatty acid emulsifier and thickener; benign and well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Stearyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: Fatty alcohol used to thicken and soften formulas; benign and well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Succinic Acid
ingredients.note: Mild dicarboxylic acid used for blemishes, oil control, and pH adjustment. Well tolerated at cosmetic levels with low irritation risk.

ingredients.name: Sunflower Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Linoleic-acid-rich, barrier-supporting emollient; safe and widely used.

ingredients.name: Superoxide Dismutase
ingredients.note: Enzymatic antioxidant that neutralizes superoxide radicals in the skin. Safe and well tolerated in topical formulations.

ingredients.name: Sweet Almond Oil
ingredients.note: Gentle, well-tolerated emollient oil; safe except for those with tree-nut allergies.

ingredients.name: Synthetic Fluorphlogopite
ingredients.note: Lab-made mica alternative used as a smooth, high-purity pearlescent filler; inert and free of the natural-mica contamination concerns.

ingredients.name: Tartaric Acid
ingredients.note: Grape-derived AHA used for exfoliation and as a pH buffer. Can increase UV sensitivity and irritate sensitive skin at high levels.

ingredients.name: Tetrahexyldecyl Ascorbate
ingredients.note: Oil-soluble, stable vitamin C ester valued for penetration and antioxidant brightening. Safe and non-irritating in formulas.

ingredients.name: Tetrahydrocurcumin
ingredients.note: Colorless curcumin derivative used as an antioxidant and brightener. Gentle and well tolerated at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Tetrasodium EDTA
ingredients.note: Alkaline tetrasodium salt of EDTA used as a metal chelator. Safe at typical low use levels; mainly an environmental persistence concern.

ingredients.name: Thioctic Acid
ingredients.note: Both fat- and water-soluble antioxidant used for smoothing and anti-aging. Well tolerated at cosmetic levels, with rare tingling at higher concentrations.

ingredients.name: Titanium dioxide
ingredients.note: Mineral sunscreen filter; safe topically (inhalation concern only for loose powders, not creams).

ingredients.name: Titanium Dioxide and Mica (Coated Micas)
ingredients.note: Mica platelets coated with titanium dioxide to create pearlescent effects. Widely approved and considered safe for cosmetic use including makeup.

ingredients.name: Tocopherol
ingredients.note: Antioxidant; safe.

ingredients.name: Tocotrienols
ingredients.note: Vitamin E family compounds with strong lipid antioxidant activity, often from palm or rice. Safe and well tolerated topically.

ingredients.name: Tranexamic Acid
ingredients.note: Topical brightener (typically 2-5%) with minimal systemic absorption and a strong cosmetic safety record. The oral form is a prescription antifibrinolytic with clot risk; only the topical use is cosmetic.

ingredients.name: Trehalose
ingredients.note: Sugar humectant valued for protecting cells against dehydration; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Tremella Mushroom Extract
ingredients.note: Polysaccharide humectant comparable to hyaluronic acid for hydration; gentle and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Trimethylsiloxysilicate
ingredients.note: Silicone resin that creates long-wear, transfer-resistant films; chemically inert and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Tripeptide-1
ingredients.note: Copper-free signal peptide that supports collagen-related skin renewal. Used at low concentrations and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Tris-Biphenyl Triazine
ingredients.note: Newer broad-spectrum micronized triazine filter (Tinosorb A2B) with a large particle size and essentially no systemic absorption. Strong central-UV coverage and a clean safety assessment.

ingredients.name: Trisodium Ethylenediamine Disuccinate
ingredients.note: Readily biodegradable chelator used as a greener alternative to EDTA. Safe and mild at use levels.

ingredients.name: Tromethamine
ingredients.note: Amino-buffer used to neutralize carbomer and stabilize pH. Considered safe at low buffering levels.

ingredients.name: Ubiquinone
ingredients.note: Lipophilic cellular antioxidant supporting mitochondrial energy and reducing oxidative damage. Safe and non-irritating in skincare.

ingredients.name: Ultramarines
ingredients.note: Synthetic sodium aluminum sulfosilicate pigments. Approved by FDA (not for lips) and EU; considered safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Urea
ingredients.note: Humectant at low levels and keratolytic at higher concentrations; safe and widely used, though high-percentage formulas can sting compromised skin.

ingredients.name: VP/Eicosene Copolymer
ingredients.note: Oil-soluble film former used for pigment binding and wear in makeup and sunscreens; regarded as safe.

ingredients.name: VP/VA Copolymer
ingredients.note: Film-forming copolymer for fixative and texturizing effects. Considered safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Xanthan Gum
ingredients.note: Polysaccharide gum from fermentation used to thicken and stabilize water phases. Natural, food-grade and very well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Xylitylglucoside
ingredients.note: Sugar-derived humectant that supports skin water reservoirs; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Zinc oxide
ingredients.note: Mineral sunscreen filter; the clean alternative to chemical UV filters. Safe.

ingredients.name: Zinc Stearate
ingredients.note: Metallic soap used as a binder and slip agent in powders; safe topically, though inhalation of loose powder forms should be avoided.
